mission: Use the actual local BuildLang/buildc corpus in C:/dev/public/pubscan/quantalang as source evidence for compiler, receipt, MIR, schematic, and documentation-maintenance benchmarks.

sources.title: BuildLang README in local pubscan checkout
observed_claims: The local checkout C:/dev/public/pubscan/quantalang is the BuildLang/buildc source tree despite the older quantalang directory name.
The README presents BuildLang as a systems language compiling .bld source to native binaries through a C backend.
The README says the crate is buildlang and the installed binary is buildc.
The README states the prior quantalang crate name is deprecated and points to buildlang/buildc.
The buildc CLI includes run, build, check, test, fmt, lint, repl, lsp, watch, pkg, mir emit/load, bdf, policy, receipt verify/export, corpus verify, doctor, and version surfaces.
The README describes buildlang.mir/v0 as a JSON interlingua and describes semantic-corpus receipt verification.
benchmark_implications: Benchmarks must preserve the local checkout alias instead of claiming files still need to be renamed.
Models must distinguish working C-backend compiler behavior from experimental or aspirational surfaces.
Documentation maintenance should be tested against CLI, MIR, receipts, and corpus gates.

sources.title: BuildLang compiler Cargo manifest
observed_claims: The compiler crate name is buildlang.
The observed local version is 1.2.0.
The binary name is buildc.
The manifest description names verified C path, shader output, experimental backends, opt-in linear types, and re-checkable accountability plus scientific-runtime receipts.
benchmark_implications: Model outputs should cite crate, version, and binary identity as verified local facts.
Release and harness docs should distinguish manifest facts from speculative roadmap claims.

sources.title: BuildLang project status
observed_claims: The working compiler core is lexer -> parser -> type checker -> MIR -> C backend -> executable.
The C backend is the only backend with end-to-end native execution verified by the compiler test suite.
The current status distinguishes working core, partial backend/tool surfaces, and aspirational self-hosted BuildLang code.
The semantic corpus checks symbol graph, MIR representation, memory layout, substrate, C/Rust execution, and check receipt surfaces.
Linear types are experimental and explicitly not a proven-sound checker.
The self-hosted compiler and stdlib are aspirational and cannot be compiled or executed today.
benchmark_implications: Benchmark responses should penalize promotion of aspirational code as production.
Benchmark responses should reward explicit backend maturity labels and known-gap preservation.
Receipt and corpus surfaces should become schematic/doc maintenance inputs.

sources.title: BuildLang scientific-runtime receipt documentation
observed_claims: buildc run --emit-receipt compiles and runs a .bld program, captures numeric stdout, checks an invariant, and emits a sealed JSON receipt.
buildc receipt verify re-runs the program, re-derives source digest, and re-checks invariant verdicts.
Receipt status includes PASS, FAIL_EXPECTED, FAIL_UNEXPECTED, and UNVERIFIABLE.
The receipt export bridge re-verifies before emitting Crucible measurement rows.
The documentation repeatedly preserves honest scope: receipts witness observed output series and do not prove physical-law correctness.
benchmark_implications: The harness should use buildc receipts as a local proof-surface template for byte-witness/accountable workflows.
Classifier-friction replacement claims should be tested through receipt semantics rather than assertion.
Documentation should bind claims to reproducible receipt verification commands.

sources.title: BuildLang VS Code extension local alias evidence
observed_claims: The local quantalang-vscode checkout applies to the buildlang-vscode repository.
The VS Code extension owns language registration, TextMate grammar, language configuration, snippets, and packaging metadata.
Compiler semantics and backend maturity live in HarperZ9/buildlang, not in the extension repo.
benchmark_implications: Documentation/schematic maintenance must keep extension surfaces separate from compiler semantics.
Agentic repo routing should avoid editing editor packaging when the requested change belongs in buildlang/buildc.

benchmark_lanes.description: Evaluate whether an agent can reason from the actual BuildLang/buildc local corpus, preserve repo aliasing, distinguish working and aspirational compiler surfaces, and turn buildc receipts/MIR/corpus verification into documentation and schematic gates.
